name: WidgetKit Widgets & Live Activities (ActivityKit)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: WidgetKit Widgets & Live Activities (ActivityKit)

FILE STRUCTURE (CRITICAL):
Widget code MUST go in a "WidgetExtension/" folder. The build system auto-creates the extension target from this folder. The main app's @main stays in App.swift; the widget's @main goes in WidgetExtension/WidgetBundle.swift.

WIDGET BASICS:
1. TimelineProvider: provides timeline entries (snapshots and full timelines).
2. TimelineEntry: a struct conforming to TimelineEntry with a `date: Date` property (required).
3. Widget view: a SwiftUI view that receives the entry.
4. Widget configuration: StaticConfiguration (no user config) or AppIntentConfiguration (iOS 17+, user-configurable).

WIDGET FILES:
- WidgetExtension/WidgetBundle.swift — @main WidgetBundle containing all widgets.
- WidgetExtension/<Name>Widget.swift — Widget struct with body: some WidgetConfiguration.
- WidgetExtension/<Name>TimelineProvider.swift — TimelineProvider implementation.
- WidgetExtension/<Name>Entry.swift — TimelineEntry struct.

TIMELINE PROVIDER PATTERN:
```
struct MyProvider: TimelineProvider {
    func placeholder(in context: Context) -> MyEntry {
        MyEntry(date: Date(), value: 0)
    }
    func getSnapshot(in context: Context, completion: @escaping (MyEntry) -> Void) {
        completion(MyEntry(date: Date(), value: 42))
    }
    func getTimeline(in context: Context, completion: @escaping (Timeline<MyEntry>) -> Void) {
        let entry = MyEntry(date: Date(), value: 42)
        let timeline = Timeline(entries: [entry], policy: .after(Date().addingTimeInterval(3600)))
        completion(timeline)
    }
}
```

WIDGET FAMILIES:
- .systemSmall, .systemMedium, .systemLarge for home screen.
- .accessoryCircular, .accessoryRectangular, .accessoryInline for lock screen (iOS 16+).
- Use .supportedFamilies([...]) on the widget configuration.
- Use Environment(\.widgetFamily) to adapt layout per size.

LIVE ACTIVITIES (ActivityKit):
1. Define ActivityAttributes in a shared file (both app and widget extension need it).
2. ActivityAttributes has an inner ContentState struct for dynamic data.
3. Start: Activity.request(attributes:content:pushType:)
4. Update: activity.update(ActivityContent(state:staleDate:))
5. End: activity.end(ActivityContent(state:staleDate:), dismissalPolicy:)
6. Widget extension provides ActivityConfiguration(for: MyAttributes.self) { context in ... } dynamicIsland: { context in ... }

DYNAMIC ISLAND:
- DynamicIsland has 4 regions: expanded (leading/trailing/center/bottom), compactLeading, compactTrailing, minimal.
- Use DynamicIslandExpandedRegion(.leading) { }, etc.

APP INTENT CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL):
- When using AppIntentConfiguration in a widget, the App Intent type (e.g. VoiceNoteIntent, ChooseFolderIntent) MUST be defined inside the WidgetExtension/ folder (e.g. WidgetExtension/VoiceNoteIntent.swift).
- The widget extension is a separate target and cannot see types from the main app. If you put the Intent in the main app only, the widget will fail with "cannot find type 'XIntent' in scope".
- Define the Intent struct in WidgetExtension/<Name>Intent.swift: import AppIntents; struct VoiceNoteIntent: WidgetConfigurationIntent { static var title: LocalizedStringResource = "Voice note"; @Parameter(title: "Note") var note: String? }.
- Use AppIntentTimelineProvider and reference the Intent type from the same WidgetExtension/ folder.

SHARED DATA:
- Use App Groups (UserDefaults(suiteName:)) or shared file containers to pass data between app and widget.
- The widget runs in a separate process; it cannot access the main app's @State or in-memory data.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- NEVER put @main on both App.swift AND WidgetBundle — only one @main per target.
- NEVER put widget code in the main app folder — it MUST be in WidgetExtension/.
- NEVER use URLSession or heavy networking directly in TimelineProvider — use cached/stored data.
- NEVER forget the `date: Date` property on TimelineEntry — it's required by the protocol.
- NEVER use NavigationStack/NavigationLink inside widgets — widgets are non-interactive views (only Link and Button with widgetURL/Link are allowed).
- NEVER reference main app types that aren't in a shared framework — the widget extension is a separate target.
- NEVER put an App Intent type used by AppIntentConfiguration in the main app — define it in WidgetExtension/ so the widget target can see it.

canonicalCode.WidgetExtension/ExampleEntry.swift:
import WidgetKit
import Foundation

struct ExampleEntry: TimelineEntry {
    let date: Date
    let value: Int
    let label: String
}

antiPatterns: Putting @main on both App.swift and WidgetBundle — only one @main per target
Placing widget code in the main app folder instead of WidgetExtension/
Using NavigationStack or NavigationLink inside widgets — widgets are non-interactive
Forgetting the required `date: Date` property on TimelineEntry
Referencing main app types directly from the widget extension without shared framework
Putting App Intent (e.g. VoiceNoteIntent) in main app when widget uses AppIntentConfiguration — define Intent in WidgetExtension/
Using heavy networking in TimelineProvider instead of cached data

commonErrors: type 'DeliveryAttributes.ContentState' does not conform to protocol 'Decodable'
type 'DeliveryAttributes.ContentState' does not conform to protocol 'Encodable'
type 'DeliveryAttributes.ContentState' does not conform to protocol 'Hashable'
type 'DeliveryAttributes.ContentState' does not conform to protocol 'Equatable'
